failed_rules.message: LoanType must be Closed End Second for 2nd Lien program
US Citizens are not eligible for Select ITIN program.
Loan amount $753,750 below minimum requirement $3,500,001
Loan amount $753,750 below minimum $4,500,001 for FICO Score 780, Primary Residence, SFR
Reserves 12 months does not meet requirement of 18 months PITIA. Note: Cash-Out proceeds may NOT be used.
Income Doc Type "Bank Stmts: 12 Mo. Personal" is not a DSCR type - Investor DSCR requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL
Occupancy 'Primary Residence' not eligible for Foreign National. Available: Investment Property, Second Home
US Citizens are not eligible for Foreign National program - FAIL
Income Doc Type "Bank Stmts: 12 Mo. Personal" is not a DSCR type - DSCR Supreme Program requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL
Income Doc Type "Bank Stmts: 12 Mo. Personal" is not a DSCR type - DSCR Multi (5-8 Units) requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL
Income Doc Type "Bank Stmts: 12 Mo. Personal" is not a DSCR type - Investor DSCR No Ratio requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL

required_doc_categories: Borrower Authorization (when NQMF is pulling credit)
LoanNex Product and Pricing Results or Completed Submission Form
Most Recent Asset Statement for Proof Reserves
Non QM Bank Statement Analysis Worksheet

_doctype_masterlist.document_type: Voluntary Information For Government Monitoring Purposes
Borrower Certification as to Business Purpose
Cover Letter for Transcript of Tax Return Form
Non QM Bank Statement Analysis Worksheet
Consent for Release of Information SSA-3288
Evidence of Delivery and Receipt of VOE

missing_core_variables.why_it_matters: Income documentation type drives all income document requirements for qualification.

income: RATIOS AND QUALIFYING – FULL AND ALT DOC

specifications: Must be valid (unexpired) government-issued photo identification
Must display borrower's full legal name matching loan application
Must include photograph, date of birth, and unique ID number
Acceptable forms: State Driver's License, State ID, US Passport, US Passport Card

reasons_needed.reason: Borrower identity verification required for all loan transactions
Name on ID must reconcile with borrower name on loan application, title, and all loan documents

specifications: Must be fully executed with all parties' signatures and dates
Must identify subject property address matching loan application
Must show purchase price consistent with loan scenario ($1,020,000 implied at 73.9% LTV for $753,750 loan)
Must include all addenda, amendments, and counteroffers
Must show earnest money deposit amount and holder
Must identify buyer name matching borrower on loan application

reasons_needed.reason: Purchase transaction requires fully executed purchase contract to establish sales price, terms, and parties
Contract required to verify arms-length transaction and identify any seller concessions or credits

specifications: Must certify borrower's intent to occupy property as primary residence
Must be signed and dated by all borrowers
Must acknowledge occupancy requirements and timeline (within 60 days of closing)
Must confirm no intent to rent or use as investment property

reasons_needed.reason: Primary residence occupancy claimed - certification required to confirm intent to occupy
Occupancy type determines LTV limits, reserve requirements, and program eligibility per OCCUPANCY REQUIREMENTS guidelines

specifications: Must include 12 consecutive months of statements
Must include all pages for each monthly statement
Must show account holder name matching borrower name
Must show financial institution name and account number or partial account number
Must show beginning and ending balance for each statement period
Must show all deposits clearly to allow deposit analysis
Must be from the account(s) that receive business income transfers
Statements must be consecutive with no missing months
reasons_needed: Bank statement income qualification requires 12 consecutive months of statements per program requirements
Qualifying income is calculated as sum of total eligible deposits divided by 12 months
Only transfers or deposits from business account(s) are considered eligible deposits for personal bank statement documentation
Account ownership must be verified to ensure income belongs to borrower
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Bank Statement Income - 12 Mo Personal Program

document_requests.applies_to: Marlene A Melendez Niccum - Business Account
specifications: Must include 12 consecutive months of statements corresponding to personal bank statement period
Must include all pages for each monthly statement
Must show business name and account number
Must show beginning and ending balance for each statement period
Must show all deposits and transfers to personal account
Must demonstrate business income source for transfers to personal account
reasons_needed: Business bank statements required to verify source of deposits into personal account
Only transfers or deposits from business account(s) are considered eligible deposits per guidelines
Business income must be traced from business account to personal account for qualification

specifications: Must be signed and dated by CPA, EA, or PTIN licensed tax preparer
The individual who signs must attest they have prepared the borrower's most recent tax returns
Must specify business expenses as a percentage of gross annual sales/revenue
Minimum 20% expense ratio required
Must cover the same period as bank statements
Must show business name consistent with loan file
reasons_needed: P&L Statement is used to calculate the expense ratio for 12-month bank statement program
Third-party prepared expense statement required unless using 50% default expense ratio
Expense ratio determines net qualifying income from gross deposits
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Bank Statement Income - Option 2: Third Party P&L Expense Statement or Option 3: Third-Party Prepared Expense Statement

document_requests.condition: If bank statements contain large or unusual deposits outside normal business income pattern
specifications: Must explain source of each large or unusual deposit
Must identify whether deposit is business income or should be excluded from calculation
Must be signed and dated by borrower
reasons_needed: Large or unusual deposits may require sourcing per underwriting guidelines
Non-business deposits (transfers, refunds, loans) must be excluded from income calculation
Deposit pattern must support qualifying income

specifications: Must cover most recent 2 consecutive months
Must include all pages including blank pages
Must show borrower name as account holder
Must show financial institution name and logo
Must show account number (may be partially masked)
Must show beginning and ending balances for each statement period
Must show complete transaction history
Must be dated within 90 days of note date
Must identify any deposits exceeding 50% of qualifying income
Must support total funds to close calculation
Must support reserve requirements
reasons_needed: Assets must be verified for funds to close per NQMF guidelines
Guidelines require assets be dated within 90 days of note date
Account ownership must be confirmed to borrower
Large deposits may require sourcing documentation
Reserve verification requires current account balances
document_requests.notes: Note: 12-month bank statements already requested under STEP_02 for income qualification. These asset verification statements may overlap but serve different verification purposes (income calculation vs. funds to close/reserves).

specifications: Must show EMD amount paid
Must show date of deposit
Must show source account for EMD funds
Must include copy of cancelled check or wire confirmation
Must show deposit was received by escrow/title company
Must reconcile to purchase contract EMD amount
Source account must be verified borrower account
reasons_needed: Purchase transaction requires verification of earnest money deposit
EMD must be sourced from acceptable funds
Funds to close calculation must account for EMD already deposited
EMD source must be verified to confirm borrower contribution
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Assets Section - Funds to Close

specifications: Must show sufficient liquid assets to cover required reserve months
Must show assets remaining after funds to close are deducted
Must identify all accounts contributing to reserves
Must show account ownership by borrower
Must be from acceptable asset sources per guidelines
Reserve calculation must support required months of PITIA
Investment and retirement accounts calculated at applicable discount rate if used
reasons_needed: Program requires reserves based on occupancy, property type, and LTV
Primary residence purchase typically requires reserve verification
Reserves must be verified from acceptable sources separate from funds to close
Reserve calculation must be supported by verified asset documentation
document_requests.notes: Reserve months required will be determined by program matrix based on LTV 73.9%, primary residence, and loan amount $753,750

specifications: Must be a tri-merge or Residential Mortgage Credit Report (RMCR) from acceptable agencies (Experian, TransUnion, Equifax)
Must include all borrowers on the loan
Must display credit scores from all three bureaus
Must show complete tradeline details including account types, balances, payment history, and dates
Must show housing/mortgage payment history if applicable
Must include all inquiries from the past 120 days
Must include public records, disputes, collections, charge-offs, and fraud alerts
Must certify results of public record searches for each city of residence in last 2 years
Must be dated within program-required recency window
Must include valid Social Security number verification
reasons_needed: Credit score of 780 must be validated to confirm program eligibility for Flex Select/Flex Supreme
Tradeline requirements (3 tradelines 12mo or 2 tradelines 24mo) must be verified per NQMF guidelines
Credit history review required to identify any credit events, inquiries, or derogatory items
Housing history verification required per program requirements
Public records must be reviewed for judgments, liens, or other adverse items affecting eligibility
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Guidelines - Credit Report section: Tri-merge or RMCR required for all borrowers
validation_rules: Minimum FICO score meets program threshold (780 reported)
Standard tradeline requirements satisfied (3 tradelines 12mo OR 2 tradelines 24mo OR 24mo mortgage 0x30x12)
No active disputes on accounts affecting qualification
No fraud alerts requiring additional verification

specifications: Must be completed on FNMA Form 1004 (Uniform Residential Appraisal Report) for single-family residence
Must identify subject property address matching loan application
Must identify property type as single-family residence and unit count
Must show appraised value to support LTV/CLTV calculation
Must be dated within 120 days of Note date; if older, recertification required
Must include all pages and addenda
Must include street map showing location of subject and all comparables
Must include exterior building sketch with dimensions and floor plan with GLA calculation
Must include original color photographs of front, street, and rear views
Must include interior photos of kitchen, all bathrooms, main living area, and any deterioration or improvements
Must include comparable sales analysis and appraiser certification
Must confirm property is in average or better condition (C4 or better; C5/C6 not acceptable)
Must confirm no health or safety violations or structural deferred maintenance
Must confirm property meets minimum standards: year-round use, contains kitchen and bathroom, highest and best use
Must identify borrower/owner information consistent with loan file
Must disclose any repairs, adverse conditions, or inspection requirements
Must include Certification and Statement of Limiting Conditions signed by appraiser
Must include copy of appraiser's E&O Insurance Policy valid as of appraisal date
reasons_needed: Collateral valuation is required for underwriting all loan transactions per NQMF guidelines
LTV of 73.9% eligibility depends on appraised value confirmation
Property type (SFR) and condition must meet program eligibility requirements
Subject property identity must be consistent across the loan file
Appraisal must confirm property meets minimum property standards
Purchase transaction requires valuation to validate sales price reasonableness

specifications: Must include FNMA SSR (Submission Summary Report) from UCDP submission
Must show SSR Score - score of 2.5 or less is acceptable per NQMF guidelines
Must identify subject property and appraisal report being reviewed
If SSR score exceeds 2.5, alternative review product required (CDA, Field Review, or second appraisal)
reasons_needed: Appraisal review process requires secondary appraisal product to support value
SSR score of 2.5 or less satisfies collateral review requirements
NQMF guidelines require appraisal validation through approved review process

specifications: Must be FEMA Flood Certificate (Standard Flood Hazard Determination Form)
Must identify subject property address
Must indicate flood zone designation (Zone A, V, or non-SFHA zone)
Must indicate whether property is in Special Flood Hazard Area (SFHA)
Must indicate NFIP community participation status if in SFHA
Must include FEMA Map Number and effective date
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require flood zone determination for all properties
Flood insurance requirement depends on FEMA SFHA designation
Properties in Zone A or V require flood insurance and NFIP community participation
Appraisal flood zone must be reconciled with flood certificate

specifications: Must be Hazard Insurance policy or Evidence of Insurance (Policy, Certificate of Insurance, or Binder)
Must name all persons holding title to subject property as named insured
Must include standard mortgagee clause (loss payable clause not acceptable)
Must protect against fire and standard extended coverage perils: fire/lightning, explosion, windstorm, hail, smoke, aircraft, vehicles, riot/civil commotion
Must provide replacement cost basis coverage (actual cash value not acceptable)
Must be effective for at least 60 days after date of funding
Must show property address matching note/security instrument
Must show policy number, loan number, coverage amounts, deductible, and premium amount
Must include insurance company name and agent information
Must include effective and expiration dates of coverage
Must require insurer notification to mortgagee before cancellation
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require hazard insurance on all properties
Collateral must be protected against fire and extended coverage hazards
Purchase transaction requires insurance evidence at closing
Mortgagee interest must be protected via standard mortgagee clause

specifications: Must identify borrower/vested parties matching Marlene A Melendez Niccum
Must identify subject property address and legal description
Must disclose all liens, judgments, easements, exceptions, and title requirements
Must show title insurer name and effective date
Must be dated within 90 days of closing
Must include 24-month chain of title history per NQMF guidelines
Must show proposed insured amount equal to or greater than loan amount ($753,750)
Title insurer must be qualified to do business in California
Must confirm first lien position without exception
reasons_needed: Title must confirm ownership and vesting align with borrower on loan application
Chain of title required to verify no flipping activity or inconsistencies per guidelines
All liens and exceptions must be reviewed to ensure clear first lien position
Title exceptions must be evaluated against allowable exceptions per NQMF guidelines
Any adverse items affecting title (judgments, liens) must be identified for payoff at closing

specifications: Must show current owner/seller name and legal property description
Must be recorded deed showing current vesting of subject property
Must match property address on purchase contract and appraisal
For purchase: seller's vesting deed confirming seller has authority to convey
Legal description must match title commitment
reasons_needed: Confirms seller has legal ownership and authority to sell property
Vesting must be reviewed against title commitment for consistency
Legal description must align across all title and property documents
Required to verify chain of title and ownership history

specifications: Must be valid (unexpired) government-issued photo identification
Must display borrower's full legal name matching loan application
Must include photograph, date of birth, and unique ID number
Acceptable forms: State Driver's License, State ID, US Passport, US Passport Card

STEP_00.summary: Parsed loan file for borrower Marlene A Melendez Niccum with loan amount $753,750, LTV 73.9%, purchase transaction for primary residence SFR in California. FICO 780. Eligibility engine confirms Flex Select and Flex Supreme are eligible programs with bank statement income documentation (12 Mo Personal). 4 documents submitted: Purchase Contract, URLA 1003, Bank Statement, and SmartFees. Documents routed to crosscutting (3), assets (1), and title_closing (1) facets. No contradictions detected. One missing core variable identified: income_documentation_type needs resolution.

STEP_01.summary: Cross-cutting document analysis completed for Marlene A Melendez Niccum purchase transaction. Generated 4 critical cross-cutting document requests: (1) Government-Issued Photo ID for identity verification, (2) IRS Form 4506-C for tax transcript authorization as required by guidelines, (3) Fully Executed Purchase Contract for this purchase transaction to establish sales price and terms, and (4) Occupancy Certification to confirm primary residence intent. No overlay conflicts detected. No contradictions or missing core variables identified that require additional resolution documents.

STEP_02.summary: Income document analysis completed for bank statement income qualification. Borrower Marlene A Melendez Niccum is self-employed using 12-month personal bank statement program. Generated 6 income document requests: (1) Personal Bank Statements - 12 consecutive months required for income calculation, (2) Business Bank Statements - 12 months to verify source of transfers to personal account, (3) Year-to-Date Profit and Loss Statement signed by CPA/EA/licensed tax preparer to establish expense ratio for income calculation, (4) Business Verification Documentation to independently verify business existence within 30 days of closing, (5) Conditional LOE for large deposits if unusual deposits exist, (6) Conditional LOE for missing bank statement months if applicable. All requests align with NQMF bank statement income guidelines.

STEP_03.summary: Asset document analysis completed for purchase transaction. Borrower Marlene A Melendez Niccum requires funds to close and reserve verification for $753,750 purchase at 73.9% LTV. Generated 3 asset document requests: (1) Personal Bank Statements - 2 consecutive months for funds to close and reserve verification (note: 12-month statements already requested for income in STEP_02 may satisfy this), (2) Earnest Money Deposit Verification - required for all purchase transactions to verify EMD source and amount, (3) Reserve Verification Documentation - to confirm sufficient liquid assets remain after funds to close. No gift funds, large deposit LOE, business bank statements, investment account statements, or retirement account statements were requested as no scenario indicators triggered these requirements. Assets must be dated within 90 days of note date per NQMF guidelines.

skip_reasons.gift_documents: No gift indicators in scenario - LTV 73.9% indicates sufficient borrower contribution
skip_reasons.business_bank_statements: Borrower using personal bank statements for income qualification
skip_reasons.investment_retirement: No investment or retirement assets explicitly listed in scenario
skip_reasons.wire_transfer_receipt: Closing logistics item - not predictive need

STEP_04.summary: Credit document analysis completed for purchase transaction. Borrower Marlene A Melendez Niccum has FICO 780 with no reported credit events. Applied strict relevance gate per guidelines: with strong credit score (780) and no credit events (BK/FC/SS), only Credit Report is required. Skipped Mortgage Payment History (purchase transaction with no existing mortgages), VOM (no existing mortgage), VOR (FICO 780 indicates established credit history on report), Tradeline Supplement (780+ FICO assumes tradeline requirements met), all credit event documentation (no events), Dispute Resolution and Fraud Alert (not speculatively included). Generated 1 credit document request total - demonstrating appropriate restraint per NQMF guidelines for clean credit profiles.

documents_skipped: Mortgage Payment History - no existing mortgages
Tradeline Supplement - FICO 780+ meets tradeline assumption
Short Sale Documentation - no SS event
Credit Event LOE - no credit events
Credit Inquiry LOE - not required for bank statement program

STEP_05.summary: Property and appraisal document analysis completed for primary residence SFR purchase in California. Applied strict relevance gate: (1) Appraisal Report - required on Form 1004 for SFR with full specifications per NQMF guidelines including 120-day recency, condition requirements (C4 or better), minimum property standards, and appraiser certification; (2) SSR/UCDP Findings - required for appraisal validation with 2.5 score threshold; (3) Flood Determination - required to establish flood zone status and potential flood insurance requirements; (4) Property Insurance - required hazard coverage with replacement cost basis and mortgagee clause. Correctly SKIPPED per relevance gate: Form 1007 Rent Schedule (primary residence, not investment), Condo Questionnaire (SFR not condo), PUD Certification (SFR not PUD), Flood Insurance (flood zone unknown - will be determined by flood certificate), 1-4 Family Rider (primary residence, not investment), Appraisal Update/Field Review (future events not predictive needs).

skip_reasons.Condo_PUD: Property type is SFR, not Condo or PUD
skip_reasons.Flood_Insurance: Flood zone unknown - determination required first
skip_reasons.Investment_docs: Primary residence occupancy - not investment property
skip_reasons.Future_events: Appraisal updates/field reviews are future events, not predictive needs

STEP_06.summary: Title and closing document analysis completed for purchase transaction. Applied strict relevance gate per NQMF guidelines: Since this is a PURCHASE transaction (not refinance), Payoff Statement and Mortgage Statement are NOT required. Property is SFR with no HOA indication, so HOA documentation is NOT required. Generated 2 title document requests: (1) Title Commitment - required to verify ownership, first lien position, exceptions, 24-month chain of title history, and title insurer qualification in California; (2) Vesting Deed - required to confirm seller's ownership and authority to convey property. Per module deduplication rules, deferred Hazard Insurance and Flood Determination to Property module (STEP_05). Did NOT request Closing Disclosure or Settlement Statement as these are closing-stage documents not available at underwriting time.

STEP_07.summary: Compliance document analysis completed for primary residence purchase transaction. Applied STRICT relevance gate per NQMF guidelines: (1) Business Purpose Affidavit NOT required - this is a primary residence purchase, NOT business purpose/DSCR/investor program, (2) ITIN Documentation NOT required - no indication borrower uses ITIN, (3) Entity/Trust Documentation NOT required - individual borrower with no entity or trust structure, (4) U.S. Citizenship Documentation NOT required - no citizenship status concerns flagged, (5) LOE for Declarations NOT required - no affirmative declarations flagged. Cross-module deduplication confirmed: Government ID, 4506-C, and Occupancy Certification already requested in STEP_01 cross-cutting module. Result: Zero additional compliance documents required for this clean primary residence loan.

STEP_08.summary: Document Request Merger and Final Output Generation completed for Marlene A Melendez Niccum purchase transaction. Merged 17 document requests from 6 modules (01-Cross-Cutting, 02-Income, 03-Assets, 04-Credit, 05-Property, 06-Title) into 14 unique document requests by deduplicating 3 overlapping requests based on document_type and context. Key merges included: Personal Bank Statements consolidated across income (12-month for income calculation) and assets (2-month for funds to close) with distinct specifications preserved for each purpose. All 14 documents have status 'needed' as no documents were found in inventory. Priority breakdown: 7 P0 (critical eligibility blockers including Bank Statements for income, Appraisal Report, Credit Report), 6 P1 (important for approval including Title documents, Hazard Insurance, CPA Letter), 1 P2 (standard required - Large Deposit Documentation). No HARD-STOP conflicts detected. Final ranked list ordered by priority then category per NQMF guidelines.

Must name all persons holding title to subject property as named insured
Must include standard mortgagee clause (loss payable clause not acceptable)
Must protect against fire and standard extended coverage perils: fire/lightning, explosion, windstorm, hail, smoke, aircraft, vehicles, riot/civil commotion
Must provide replacement cost basis coverage (actual cash value not acceptable)
Must be effective for at least 60 days after date of funding
Must show property address matching note/security instrument
Must show policy number, loan number, coverage amounts, deductible, and premium amount
Must include insurance company name and agent information
Must include effective and expiration dates of coverage
Must require insurer notification to mortgagee before cancellation
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require hazard insurance on all properties
Collateral must be protected against fire and extended coverage hazards
Purchase transaction requires insurance evidence at closing
Mortgagee interest must be protected via standard mortgagee clause

specifications: Must show current owner/seller name and legal property description
Must be recorded deed showing current vesting of subject property
Must match property address on purchase contract and appraisal
For purchase: seller's vesting deed confirming seller has authority to convey
Legal description must match title commitment
reasons_needed: Confirms seller has legal ownership and authority to sell property
Vesting must be reviewed against title commitment for consistency
Legal description must align across all title and property documents
Required to verify chain of title and ownership history

specifications: Must explain source of each large or unusual deposit
Must identify whether deposit is business income or should be excluded from calculation
Must be signed and dated by borrower
reasons_needed: Large or unusual deposits may require sourcing per underwriting guidelines
Non-business deposits (transfers, refunds, loans) must be excluded from income calculation
Deposit pattern must support qualifying income
